title: echinacea pallida var. tennesseensis
intro: Echinacea pallida var. tennesseensis, also known as Tennessee coneflower, is a subspecies of the Echinacea pallida plant, native to the southeastern United States. It is a perennial herb that has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Echinacea pallida var. tennesseensis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of the common cold and flu, as well as its ability to boost the body's natural defenses against infection. Echinacea pallida var. tennesseensis can be consumed in various herbal preparations, such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are made by infusing the plant's roots and flowers in a solvent like water or alcohol.
